2604.18249.title: Where Do Self-Supervised Speech Models Become Unfair?
2604.18249.paper_summary: Speech encoder models are known to model members of some speaker groups (SGs) better than others. However, there has been little work in establishing why this occurs on a technological level. To our knowledge, we present the first layerwise fairness analysis of pretrained self-supervised speech encoder models (S3Ms), probing each embedding layer for speaker identification (SID) automatic speech recognition (ASR). We find S3Ms produce embeddings biased against certain SGs for both tasks, starting at the very first latent layers. Furthermore, we find opposite patterns of layerwise bias for SID vs ASR for all models in our study: SID bias is minimized in layers that minimize overall SID error; on the other hand, ASR bias is maximized in layers that minimize overall ASR error. The inverse bias/error relationship for ASR is unaffected when probing S3Ms that are finetuned for ASR, suggesting SG-level bias is established during pretraining and is difficult to remove.
2604.18249.paper_summary_zh: 語音編碼模型被認為對某些說話者群體（SGs）的成員建模效果優於其他群體。然而，對於為什麼在技術層面上會發生這種情況的研究相對較少。據我們所知，我們首次對預訓練的自我監督語音編碼模型（S3Ms）進行了逐層公平性分析，探討每個嵌入層在說話者識別（SID）和自動語音識別（ASR）中的表現。我們發現S3Ms在這兩個任務中對某些SGs產生了偏見的嵌入，這種偏見從最初的潛在層開始。此外，我們發現在我們研究的所有模型中，SID與ASR的逐層偏見呈現相反的模式：在最小化整體SID錯誤的層中，SID偏見被最小化；另一方面，在最小化整體ASR錯誤的層中，ASR偏見被最大化。對於ASR的反向偏見/錯誤關係在探測為ASR微調的S3Ms時不受影響，這表明SG級別的偏見是在預訓練期間建立的，並且難以去除。
2604.18249.author: Felix Herron et.al.
2604.18249.authors: Felix Herron, Maja Hjuler, Solange Rossato, Alexandre Allauzen, François Portet
